name: Procedural
limitation_note: general symbolic structure may remain implicit
historical_note: important in ancient mathematical problem traditions

name: Rhetorical
limitation_note: long expressions can obscure repeated form
historical_note: common in early algebraic texts and equation classifications

name: Syncopated
limitation_note: not yet a full symbolic system
historical_note: associated with Diophantine and cossic abbreviation traditions

name: Symbolic
limitation_note: requires learned conventions and hidden domain assumptions
historical_note: strongly associated with Viète, Descartes, and early modern algebra

name: Structural
mathematical_effect: represents algebraic systems rather than only numbers
limitation_note: abstraction can obscure examples and access
historical_note: central to nineteenth- and twentieth-century abstract algebra

name: Logical and set-theoretic
mathematical_effect: formalizes claims, membership, relations, and inference
limitation_note: symbol density can hide meaning for learners
historical_note: important in algebra of logic, set theory, and foundations

name: Computational
dominant_medium: expression trees, parser syntax, formal languages
mathematical_effect: turns symbols into data structures for computation
limitation_note: software syntax and mathematical notation can diverge
historical_note: central to computer algebra, programming languages, and proof assistants
